seoTitle: How to Add and Customize Tables in Google Docs
metaDescription: Insert a table in Google Docs, then merge cells, adjust borders, and set background colors using the Table Properties sidebar.
intro: Organizing data or building a comparison grid in Google Docs is easy once you know the table tools beyond the basic insert grid. This guide covers inserting a table, adding or merging cells, and customizing colors and borders.

steps.heading: Insert a table
steps.body: Click Insert, hover over Table, and drag across the grid selector to choose your number of rows and columns.

steps.heading: Fill in your data
steps.body: Click into each cell to type your content, using Tab to move between cells quickly.

steps.heading: Add or remove rows and columns
steps.body: Right-click inside a cell and choose 'Insert row below' or 'Insert column right' to expand the table as needed.

steps.heading: Merge cells for headers
steps.body: Highlight multiple cells, right-click, and select 'Merge cells' to create a clean header row.

steps.heading: Customize colors and borders
steps.body: Go to Format, hover over Table, and select Table Properties to adjust background color, padding, and border thickness.

faq.question: Can I resize columns by dragging?
faq.answer: Yes, hover over a column border until the cursor changes, then drag to adjust its width.

faq.question: How do I delete an entire table?
faq.answer: Right-click inside the table and select 'Delete table' from the context menu.

faq.question: Can I sort table rows alphabetically?
faq.answer: Google Docs doesn't offer native table sorting, so a sorting add-on or copying data into Sheets is the usual workaround.

faq.question: Will a large table split across pages properly?
faq.answer: Yes, Google Docs automatically handles page breaks within tables, though you can adjust row settings if it splits awkwardly.
